Divino Afflatu - 1954/2024-03-28/Complin.citation: upstream/web/cgi-bin/horas/horasscripts.pl:638 + officium.pl:186 — Perl emits the bracketed psalm-slot index from the global counters `$psalmnum1`/`$psalmnum2`, which are initialized once per officium.pl load and only re-zeroed inside `psalmi()`. The DA Triduum `Special Completorium` bypasses `psalmi()` (the `[Special Completorium]` block replaces the `#Psalmi` slot directly), so the counter retains the value left over from the first invocation of `collect_units` in `packages/compositor/test/fixtures/officium-content-snapshot.pl:74-103`. The harness calls `collect_units` once per language without resetting the counter, so the Latin pass starts at the post-English counter (4 for DA Maundy/Good Friday) and renders Psalm 4 as `[5]`. The compositor's `[4]` reflects the psalm number doubling as the bracket label in `packages/compositor/src/compose/triduum-special.ts:309`. Both renderings preserve the same `Psalmus 4` heading and identical psalm verses; the bracket index is a non-authoritative display counter that the source does not prescribe.
Divino Afflatu - 1954/2024-03-28/Complin.notes: Fan out to all DA Triduum Special Completorium rows where Perl's bracket counter diverges from the compositor's psalm-number bracket.

Divino Afflatu - 1954/2024-03-28/Lauds/9.firstActual: 50:3a Miserére mei, Deus, * secúndum magnam misericórdiam tuam.
Divino Afflatu - 1954/2024-03-28/Lauds/9.citation: upstream/web/www/horas/Latin/Psalterium/Psalmorum/Psalm50.txt:1 — the Psalm 50 corpus encodes verse 3a as `Miserére mei, Deus, * secúndum magnam misericórdiam tuam.` The compositor preserves that source-backed first-verse rendering with the inline 50:3a numeric marker; the Perl comparison surface omits that line entirely (renders blank `_`) on DA Triduum Lauds and minor hours after the source-backed silent prayers.
Divino Afflatu - 1954/2024-03-28/Lauds/9.notes: DA Triduum Lauds / minor-hour rendering family where Perl emits a blank `_` line where the compositor preserves the source-backed Psalm 50:3a opening verse.

Divino Afflatu - 1954/2024-03-28/Matins/.citation: upstream/web/www/horas/Latin/Sancti/01-06.txt:4-8 — the Epiphany Rule explicitly says `Omit ad Matutinum Incipit Invitatorium Hymnus`, so Matins opens directly at the nocturn heading. The compositor preserves that source-backed omission while the Perl comparison surface still emits the suppressed pre-lesson opener.
Divino Afflatu - 1954/2024-03-28/Matins/.notes: Representative entry for the Divino Afflatu Epiphany-octave Matins omit family where Perl keeps the suppressed opener and the source-backed compositor starts at `Nocturnus I`.

Divino Afflatu - 1954/2024-03-29/None/97.notes: DA Triduum Lauds / minor-hour rendering family where Perl emits a blank `_` line where the compositor preserves the source-backed Psalm 50:3a opening verse.

Divino Afflatu - 1954/2024-03-29/Vespers.firstExpected: 115:7 Dirupísti víncula mea: * tibi sacrificábo hóstiam laudis, et nomen Dómini invocábo.
Divino Afflatu - 1954/2024-03-29/Vespers.firstActual: 115:7 Dirupísti víncula mea: ‡ tibi sacrificábo hóstiam laudis, * et nomen Dómini invocábo.
Divino Afflatu - 1954/2024-03-29/Vespers.citation: upstream/web/www/horas/Latin/Psalterium/Psalmorum/Psalm115.txt:7 — the source line explicitly encodes Psalm 115:7b as `Dirupísti víncula mea: ‡ (8) tibi sacrificábo hóstiam laudis, * et nomen Dómini invocábo.` The compositor preserves that half-verse structure while Perl flattens it to a single `*` boundary.
Divino Afflatu - 1954/2024-03-29/Vespers.notes: Fanout of the Psalm 115 half-verse marker family for the DA Triduum Vespers row exposed by the new prelude rubric emit.

Divino Afflatu - 1954/2024-03-30/Complin.citation: upstream/web/www/horas/Latin/Psalterium/Common/Rubricae.txt:168 — the corpus rubric carries guillemets around `Jube, Dómine, benedícere;`. Perl strips them while the compositor preserves them; both are surface renderings of the same rubric sentence, not a liturgical-content disagreement.
Divino Afflatu - 1954/2024-03-30/Complin.notes: Representative entry for the Divino Afflatu single-reciter Compline rubric. Fan out to all matching DA Compline rows with the same first-divergence text.

Divino Afflatu - 1954/2024-11-01/Complin.firstExpected: Confíteor Deo omnipoténti, beátæ Maríæ semper Vírgini, beáto Michaéli Archángelo, beáto Joánni Baptístæ, sanctis Apóstolis Petro et Paulo, et ómnibus Sanctis, quia peccávi nimis, cogitatióne, verbo et ópere: percutit sibi pectus mea culpa, mea culpa, mea máxima culpa. Ídeo precor beátam Maríam semper Vírginem, beátum Michaélem Archángelum, beátum Joánnem Baptístam, sanctos Apóstolos Petrum et Paulum, et omnes Sanctos, oráre pro me ad Dóminum Deum nostrum.
Divino Afflatu - 1954/2024-11-01/Complin.firstActual: Extra Chorum, quando ab uno tantum recitatur Officium dicitur: «Jube, Dómine, benedícere;» et subjungitur congruens Benedictio.
Divino Afflatu - 1954/2024-11-01/Complin.citation: upstream/web/www/horas/Latin/Sancti/11-02.txt:7-19 + upstream/web/www/horas/Latin/Commune/C9.txt — Office of the Dead under DA includes Matins, Lauds, and Vespers but **no Compline section** (C9 lacks any Compline slots; 11-02's Rule says `Omit Incipit Hymnus Capitulum Lectio Preces Commemoratio Suffragium`, with `(rubrica 1955 aut rubrica 1960) No prima Vespera` gating the no-prima-Vespera rule out under DA). On Nov 1 evening DA anticipates First Vespers of All Souls, but the Office of the Dead carries no Compline rubrics, so Compline that follows is of the day (All Saints). The compositor honors that and emits the standard All Saints Compline opening (`[Lectio brevis]` rubric → `$Adjutorium nostrum` → `$Pater noster` → `$Confiteor` …). Perl applies the Office-of-the-Dead `Omit Incipit` modifications to Compline anyway and replaces the entire Roman Compline incipit with the bare Confiteor line plus Réquiem ætérnam V/R after each psalm and a `Conclusio specialis` ending — a hybrid that has no corpus authority. Net: every Compline line shifts; the divergence is the entire pre-Confiteor opening (Lectio Brevis, Adjutorium, Pater Noster) the compositor preserves and Perl drops.
Divino Afflatu - 1954/2024-11-01/Complin.notes: DA-only family. Reduced 1955 / Rubrics 1960 set `No prima Vespera` so they do not anticipate Office of the Dead and Nov 1 Compline is just All Saints Compline on both sides. Stable across years — any DA-equivalent policy where Office of the Dead First Vespers is anticipated will exhibit the same Compline divergence.

Rubrics 1960 - 1960/2024-01-01/Lauds/2af.firstExpected: 99:3 Pópulus ejus, et oves páscuæ ejus: * introíte portas ejus in confessióne, átria ejus in hymnis: confitémini illi.
Rubrics 1960 - 1960/2024-01-01/Lauds/2af.firstActual: 99:3 Pópulus ejus, et oves páscuæ ejus: ‡ introíte portas ejus in confessióne, * átria ejus in hymnis: confitémini illi.
Rubrics 1960 - 1960/2024-01-01/Lauds/2af.citation: upstream/web/www/horas/Latin/Psalterium/Psalmorum/Psalm99.txt:3-5 — the psalm corpus explicitly encodes 99:3b as `Pópulus ejus, et oves páscuæ ejus: ‡ (4a) introíte portas ejus in confessióne, * átria ejus in hymnis: confitémini illi.` The compositor preserves that half-verse structure while removing the numeric carry marker; the Perl comparison surface flattens the same line to a single `*` split.
Rubrics 1960 - 1960/2024-01-01/Lauds/2af.notes: Representative entry for the Rubrics 1960 Lauds Psalm 99 half-verse family where the corpus keeps a `‡ ... *` structure and Perl flattens it to a single `*` boundary.

Rubrics 1960 - 1960/2024-01-01/Lauds/9fe.firstActual: Ant. Allelúja, * Dóminus regnávit, decórem índuit, allelúja, allelúja.
Rubrics 1960 - 1960/2024-01-01/Lauds/9fe.citation: Phase 3 plan §3h — the compositor does not emit `Psalmus N [M]` heading lines before each psalm in psalmody sections. Perl emits one per psalm at every Hour with psalmody. The fix lives in `packages/compositor/src/emit/sections.ts` on the psalmody-slot emission path and is deferred within §3h as a structural change; see ADJUDICATION_LOG.md for the pattern catalogue.
Rubrics 1960 - 1960/2024-01-01/Lauds/9fe.notes: Representative entry for the missing-psalm-heading pattern. Affects Lauds, Prime, Terce, Sext, None, Vespers, Compline on nearly every date across every policy. Bulk-populate once the fix lands and remaining rows are re-classified.

Rubrics 1960 - 1960/2024-04-06/Vespers/4.firstActual: Ant. Benedíctus Dóminus * suscéptor meus et liberátor meus, allelúja.
Rubrics 1960 - 1960/2024-04-06/Vespers/4.citation: upstream/web/www/horas/Latin/Psalterium/Psalmi/Psalmi major.txt:142-147 — the Day6 Vespera psalter source carries `Benedíctus Dóminus * suscéptor meus et liberátor meus.;;143(1-8)` for the Saturday Vespers first slot; the paschaltide add-alleluia HourDirective appends `, allelúja.` per the standard paschaltide decoration applied throughout the Hour. Tempora/Pasc0-6 has `No secunda Vespera`, so 2024-04-06 Vespers is First Vespers of Pasc1-0 (Dominica in Albis); Pasc1-0 has no proper `[Ant Vespera]` and inherits the Day6 Vespera psalter row for the Saturday-evening psalmody. The compositor emits that source-backed antiphon with paschaltide decoration; the Perl comparison surface fires `alleluia_ant` (`upstream/web/cgi-bin/horas/specials/psalmi.pl:604-622` checks `!exists($winner{Ant Vespera})` and substitutes a single triple-alleluia antiphon, stripping antiphons from positions 1, 2, 3, and -1).
Rubrics 1960 - 1960/2024-04-06/Vespers/4.notes: Representative entry for the shared Roman paschaltide First Vespers psalmody-antiphon family (parallel to the 2024-04-07 Low Sunday Lauds Day0 Laudes1 paschal antiphon family). Fan out to matching Roman rows with the same first-divergence pair.

Rubrics 1960 - 1960/2024-12-26/Vespers/c.citation: upstream/web/www/horas/Latin/Sancti/12-26.txt:9-14 — the St Stephen feast carries an unconditional [Rule] line `no Psalm5` instructing the engine to omit the 5th Vespers psalm under any policy. The compositor honors that source-backed rule and emits 4 antiphons / 4 psalms before transitioning to the capitulum (`Act. 6:8`). Rubrics 1960 - 1960/2024-12-26/Vespers/c.notes: Representative entry for the St Stephen Vespers `no Psalm5` family where Perl ignores the source-backed rule that limits the Vespers psalmody to 4 psalms.

Rubrics 1960 - 1960/2026-04-11/Vespers/4.citation: upstream/web/www/horas/Latin/Psalterium/Psalmi/Psalmi major.txt:142-147 — the Day6 Vespera psalter source carries `Benedíctus Dóminus * suscéptor meus et liberátor meus.;;143(1-8)` for the Saturday Vespers first slot; the paschaltide add-alleluia HourDirective appends `, allelúja.` per the standard paschaltide decoration applied throughout the Hour. Tempora/Pasc0-6 has `No secunda Vespera`, so 2024-04-06 Vespers is First Vespers of Pasc1-0 (Dominica in Albis); Pasc1-0 has no proper `[Ant Vespera]` and inherits the Day6 Vespera psalter row for the Saturday-evening psalmody. The compositor emits that source-backed antiphon with paschaltide decoration; the Perl comparison surface fires `alleluia_ant` (`upstream/web/cgi-bin/horas/specials/psalmi.pl:604-622` checks `!exists($winner{Ant Vespera})` and substitutes a single triple-alleluia antiphon, stripping antiphons from positions 1, 2, 3, and -1).

Rubrics 1960 - 1960/2026-04-18/Vespers/4.citation: upstream/web/www/horas/Latin/Psalterium/Psalmi/Psalmi major.txt:142-147 — the Day6 Vespera psalter source carries `Benedíctus Dóminus * suscéptor meus et liberátor meus.;;143(1-8)` for the Saturday Vespers first slot; the paschaltide add-alleluia HourDirective appends `, allelúja.` per the standard paschaltide decoration applied throughout the Hour. Tempora/Pasc0-6 has `No secunda Vespera`, so 2024-04-06 Vespers is First Vespers of Pasc1-0 (Dominica in Albis); Pasc1-0 has no proper `[Ant Vespera]` and inherits the Day6 Vespera psalter row for the Saturday-evening psalmody. The compositor emits that source-backed antiphon with paschaltide decoration; the Perl comparison surface fires `alleluia_ant` (`upstream/web/cgi-bin/horas/specials/psalmi.pl:604-622` checks `!exists($winner{Ant Vespera})` and substitutes a single triple-alleluia antiphon, stripping antiphons from positions 1, 2, 3, and -1).

Rubrics 1960 - 1960/2026-05-09/Vespers/4.firstActual: Ant. Benedíctus Dóminus * suscéptor meus et liberátor meus, allelúja.
Rubrics 1960 - 1960/2026-05-09/Vespers/4.citation: upstream/web/www/horas/Latin/Psalterium/Psalmi/Psalmi major.txt:142-147 — the Day6 Vespera psalter source carries `Benedíctus Dóminus * suscéptor meus et liberátor meus.;;143(1-8)` for the Saturday Vespers first slot; the paschaltide add-alleluia HourDirective appends `, allelúja.` per the standard paschaltide decoration applied throughout the Hour. Tempora/Pasc0-6 has `No secunda Vespera`, so 2024-04-06 Vespers is First Vespers of Pasc1-0 (Dominica in Albis); Pasc1-0 has no proper `[Ant Vespera]` and inherits the Day6 Vespera psalter row for the Saturday-evening psalmody. The compositor emits that source-backed antiphon with paschaltide decoration; the Perl comparison surface fires `alleluia_ant` (`upstream/web/cgi-bin/horas/specials/psalmi.pl:604-622` checks `!exists($winner{Ant Vespera})` and substitutes a single triple-alleluia antiphon, stripping antiphons from positions 1, 2, 3, and -1).
Rubrics 1960 - 1960/2026-05-09/Vespers/4.notes: Representative entry for the shared Roman paschaltide First Vespers psalmody-antiphon family (parallel to the 2024-04-07 Low Sunday Lauds Day0 Laudes1 paschal antiphon family). Fan out to matching Roman rows with the same first-divergence pair.

Rubrics 1960 - 1960/2026-05-31/Prime/c06.citation: upstream/web/www/horas/Latin/Tempora/Pent01-0.txt:332-333 supplies `[Lectio Prima] @Psalterium/Special/Minor Special:Dominica NonaOP:1-2`; upstream/web/www/horas/Latin/Psalterium/Special/Minor Special.txt:61-64 defines `[Dominica NonaOP]` as `!1 Joann. 5:7 / v. Tres sunt, qui testimónium dant in cælo: Pater, Verbum et Spíritus Sanctus, et hi tres unum sunt.`. Codex Rubricarum (1960) §191 keeps the proper feast-day Prime capitulum when the office supplies one (and the parallel pre-1960 rubric is identical), so the Festum Sanctissimæ Trinitatis correctly carries `1 Joann. 5:7`. The Perl comparison surface keeps the weekday `1 Tim. 1:17` chapter, dropping the source-backed Trinity proper Prime lesson.
Rubrics 1960 - 1960/2026-05-31/Prime/c06.notes: Representative entry for the shared Roman Trinity Sunday proper Prime capitulum family where the feast file's `[Lectio Prima]` overrides the weekday `Regi sæculorum` chapter. Fan out to matching Roman rows on Trinity Sunday with the same first-divergence pair.

Rubrics 1960 - 1960/2026-10-24/Compline/.notes: Rubrics 1960 2026 ordinary Sunday First Vespers Compline witness; 2026-10-24 remains Saturday Compline under Breviary 1960 no. 166, distinct from festive/semifestive First Vespers.

Allelúja, * allelúja, allelúja.`.

Allelúja, * allelúja, allelúja.`.
Rubrics 1960 - 1960/2026-04-16/Vespers/c.notes: Rubrics 1960 2026 Paschaltide temporal/Ascension antiphon fanout. The source-backed compositor keeps the concrete psalter or proper antiphon and applies Paschaltide decoration; Perl collapses the first comparison line to a generic triple-Alleluia antiphon.
